text: Git branching is a powerful feature of version control that helps teams work on code without interfering with each other. Instead of making changes directly to the main codebase, developers create feature branches. Each branch is a separate copy of the project where you can experiment, fix bugs, or add new features. Once the work on a branch is complete, it can be merged back into the main branch. This merging process combines the changes from different branches. In many workflows, a pull request is used to propose a merge. Other team members review the code, discuss improvements, and approve it before integration. This structured process ensures a smooth version control workflow.
